remarkList.remark:
Operator comments:

Marquis and Pole keep operations interesting. This round we added 4 interim ports with 1:1 freight paid A-C. At the first interim (Huelva) Marquis needed to denature the ethanol in shore tanks for reload and further carriage to Immingham. Delays in Thames required us to rearrange the bunkers in Amsterdam or the barge would have sailed before we were scheduled to berth. Via agents we rented a dolphin ($8k USD) for 48H where the stem was delivered, only today we were informed that the cost was waived given vessel berthed directly from there. Thanks to Karolina for quick communication in the first instance. The financial improvements make the constant changes and and queries easier to digest. The deviation and interim port claim was invoiced at $993k thanks in part to the $46kpd COA demurrage rate. 72% of the increased bunker costs are included in this claim while the balance is for us due extended time in the 1:1 base ports on LSMGO. Vessel had slops removed ($4k) and chemicals replenished ($5k) in Amsterdam which contribute to added expenses. She's now ballasting back to St. Rose to lift the 6th cargo for Marquis, ETA currently 20 June but monitoring Master's health as he was diagnosed by Radio Medic to have suffered a minor stroke onboard. Planning to change command on arrival SW. Pass and send him ashore for evaluation in a hospital.
Port times updated Europe - interim port time/bunkers updated as per actuals for first 3 ports
second call to thames reinstated - added deviation and interim port time/bunkers
2nd call to Thames cancelled reducing interim port days/bunkers
backloading confirmed in Huelva - denatured ethanol to be delivered to Immingham (final disport) - additional deviation time and bunkers + interim port time and bunkers updated.
Marquis inquired about a discharge (undenatured) / reload (denatured) of Ethanol in Huelva
mooring/unmooring daylight restricted due high river stage
AB walked off ship in NOLA - no contact with him after 23 APR...
added 3 interim ports estimation (Huelva/Thames/Amsterdam) - 1:1 = St. Rose/Thamesport
tank cleaning about 25k less than estimated - SlopW coating touched up and cured en route
